description: Characterization of thuricin CD biosynthesis - a two-component sactipeptide antibiotic with unexpected enzyme cooperation mechanisms

TrnC.name: TrnC rSAM enzyme
TrnC.catalytic_role: Catalytically active - contains [4Fe-4S] cluster essential for modification
TrnC.core_peptide_interaction: rSAM active site has greater access to core peptide

TrnD.name: TrnD rSAM enzyme
TrnD.catalytic_role: Catalytically inactive in complex - cluster mutation has no effect on activity
TrnD.leader_peptide_interaction: RRE preferentially binds TrnA/B leader peptides

in_vitro_binding_assays.substrate: Both TrnA and TrnB precursor peptides
in_vitro_binding_assays.complex_formation: TrnC and TrnD form tight heterodimer

rre_deletion_study.interpretation: RRE-RRE interaction critical for complex formation
rre_deletion_study.novelty: First demonstration of RRE in enzyme-enzyme dimerization

in_vitro_activity_assays.note: Can cleave SAM (in vitro side-reaction) but no peptide modification
Can cleave SAM (in vitro side-reaction) but no peptide modification

in_vitro_activity_assays.result: Complete modification of both TrnA and TrnB
Complete modification of both TrnA and TrnB

in_vitro_activity_assays.implication: Only TrnC cluster required for catalysis

heterodimerization.position: Both RRE domains meet at heterodimeric interface

leader_peptide_binding.secondary_position: TrnA core peptide interacts with TrnC catalytic core

catalytic_site.access: Greater access to C-terminal core peptide modification sites
catalytic_site.cluster_position: [4Fe-4S]1+ clusters of both TrnC and TrnD indicated

unexpected_findings.implication: Cannot predict from gene sequence information alone
unexpected_findings.discovery_context: Contrary to expectations based on two-component lanthipeptides

unexpected_findings.significance: TrnD catalytically inactive but functionally essential
unexpected_findings.implication: New paradigm for rSAM enzyme cooperation

unexpected_findings.significance: RREs not limited to substrate recognition
unexpected_findings.implication: RREs have broader functional roles than previously appreciated

unexpected_findings.significance: Both leader peptides bind to TrnD RRE, not TrnC
unexpected_findings.mechanism: Despite exposed RREs on both enzymes

catalytic_efficiency.cooperative_effect: Only active on peptide substrate in heterodimer

modification_yield.with_both_enzymes: Complete modification of both precursor peptides

cross_link_formation_details.specificity: Linking C-terminal cysteine residues to N-terminal core region
cross_link_formation_details.product: Nested thioether rings in mature TrnA and TrnB peptides

known_two_component_systems.difference_from_thuricin: Each precursor modified by single enzyme
Each precursor modified by single enzyme
Each precursor modified by single enzyme

comparative_analysis.thuricin_cd_novelty: First sactipeptide with two-component system and cooperative rSAM enzymes

challenges: Cannot predict functional specificity from gene sequence alone
Enzyme-enzyme interactions critical but difficult to predict
Biophysical parameters (Kd) must be determined experimentally
recommendations: Detailed mechanistic characterization before engineering attempts
Validation of AF3 predictions with experimental structures
Exploration of RRE roles in other RiPP systems

key_discoveries_summary.breakthrough_1: First demonstration of obligatory rSAM enzyme heteromerization
key_discoveries_summary.breakthrough_2: Asymmetric catalytic roles in two-component RiPP system
key_discoveries_summary.breakthrough_4: Complexity of precursor peptide recognition by enzyme complex
